title: A Novel Approach to Quantify Heterogeneity of Intrahepatic Cholangiocarcinoma: the Hidden-Genome Classifier
text:
PMID: 38864854 · DOI: 10.1158/1078-0432.CCR-24-0657 · Journal: Clinical Cancer Research (2024)


Song et al. applied a supervised machine-learning “hidden-genome” classifier to 527 intrahepatic cholangiocarcinomas (IHC) sequenced by MSK-IMPACT, scoring each tumor’s genetic similarity to extrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma/gallbladder cancer (EHC/GBC) versus hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC). IHC genetics form a spectrum: “biliary-class” IHC (>90% EHC/GBC homology, 23%) is KRAS/SMAD4/CDKN2A-driven and has markedly worse overall survival than “HCC-class” IHC (>90% HCC homology, 5.7%, TERT-driven), and the classifier predicts OS independently of FGFR2 and IDH1 alterations and outperforms histologic subtyping PMID:38864854.




1,370 patients with histologically confirmed biliary tract cancer, HCC, or biphenotypic IHC/HCC tumors at MSK, sequenced 2003–2022 on MSK-IMPACT (panels 341/410/468/505; 341 common genes used).
527 IHCH classified; trained against EHCH, GBC, and HCC reference classes.
Dataset: hcc_msk_2024.





410 IHC (78%) had >50% genetic homology with EHC/GBC; 122 (23%) exceeded >90% homology and were labeled “biliary-class” PMID:38864854.
117 IHC (22%) had >50% genetic homology with HCC; 30 (5.7%) exceeded >90% homology and were labeled “HCC-class” PMID:38864854.
Biliary-class IHC was characterized by alterations in KRAS, SMAD4, and CDKN2A loss; HCC-class was characterized by TERT alterations PMID:38864854.
Median OS, unresectable disease: biliary- vs. non-biliary-class = 1.0 year (95% CI 0.77–1.5) vs. 1.8 years (95% CI 1.6–2.0) PMID:38864854.
Median OS, resectable disease: biliary- vs. non-biliary-class = 2.4 years (95% CI 2.1–NR) vs. 5.1 years (95% CI 4.8–6.9) PMID:38864854.
Large-duct IHC (n=28) was almost exclusively biliary-class (n=27); HCC-class tumors were mostly small-duct IHC (64%, p=0.02) PMID:38864854.
The hidden-genome classifier predicted OS independently of FGFR2 and IDH1 alterations; histologic subtype did not predict OS PMID:38864854.





KRAS — enriched in biliary-class IHC; discriminative of EHC/GBC-like genetics.
SMAD4 — enriched in biliary-class IHC.
CDKN2A — loss enriched in biliary-class IHC.
TERT — alterations characterize HCC-class IHC.
FGFR2 — actionable driver; classifier adds prognostic information independent of FGFR2 status.
IDH1 — actionable driver; classifier prognostic independent of IDH1 status.





The hidden-genome classifier stratifies IHC into prognostic subgroups (biliary- vs. HCC-class) with large OS differences in both resectable and unresectable disease, and does so independently of the established FGFR2/IDH1 targetable alterations PMID:38864854.
Authors propose using the classifier to stratify patients in future IHC clinical trials and to help explain heterogeneous treatment responses in IHC PMID:38864854.





Single-institution retrospective MSK cohort; restricted to the 341 genes common across MSK-IMPACT panel versions.
Classifier was trained on EHC/GBC vs. HCC reference classes only; intermediate/mixed IHCs (roughly half the cohort) are not cleanly captured by the >90% homology thresholds.
Prospective validation and linkage to specific therapy responses (e.g., gemcitabine/cisplatin, FGFR/IDH1 inhibitors, immunotherapy) remain open.





“410 IHC (78%) had >50% genetic homology with EHC/GBC; 122 (23%) had >90% homology (‘biliary-class’), characterized by alterations of KRAS, SMAD4, and CDKN2A loss.” (Abstract)
“117 IHC (22%) had >50% genetic homology with HCC; 30 (5.7%) had >90% homology (‘HCC-class’), characterized by TERT alterations.” (Abstract)
“The hidden-genomic classifier predicted OS independent of FGFR2 and IDH1 alterations. By contrast, the histology subtype did not predict OS.” (Abstract)

title: RNA sequencing
text:
Bulk RNA sequencing of tumor (and where available matched normal) tissue for gene expression quantification, fusion detection, and downstream signature/classifier analysis.




PMID:35927489 — RNA-seq on n=712 CLL samples (603 treatment-naive used for clustering); unsupervised clustering identified 8 robust expression clusters that are independent prognostic factors beyond IGHV/epitype PMID:35927489.
PMID:36862133 — select targeted RNA sequencing alongside MSK-IMPACT in the Make-an-IMPACT program; detected actionable fusions including a PRDX1-NTRK1 fusion in histiocytosis only via targeted RNA-seq, plus MS4A6A-BRAF, DOCK8-BRAF, HLA-A-BRAF, and TFG-ALK PMID:36862133.
PMID:37202560 — bulk RNA-seq on 348 AC-ICAM colon cancer tumor/normal pairs; underpins the ICR signature, ConsensusTME deconvolution, and CMS classification used for prognostic analysis PMID:37202560.





In the CLL-map study, RNA-seq is one arm of an integrated WES/WGS + RNA-seq + methylation analysis used to derive an integrated outcome model PMID:35927489.
In the AC-ICAM atlas, RNA-seq drives both immune signature scoring and downstream microbiome integration PMID:37202560.

title: PhylogicNDT
text:
Phylogenetic and clonal-dynamics inference toolkit for reconstructing tumor evolution and the relative temporal order of somatic events from bulk sequencing.




PMID:35927489 — used in the CLL-map cohort for temporal ordering of driver events: trisomy 12 was placed early; TP53/NOTCH1 intermediate in both IGHV subtypes; BRAF early in M-CLL but late in U-CLL (q<0.1); MYD88 early in M-CLL; 20p loss and FUBP1 potentially initiating in U-CLL PMID:35927489.





Applied to CLL WES/WGS to derive subtype-specific evolutionary trajectories distinguishing M-CLL from U-CLL PMID:35927489.

title: Targeted DNA sequencing
text:
Targeted DNA sequencing uses hybrid-capture or amplicon panels to deeply sequence a predefined set of loci, enabling sensitive detection of low-allele-fraction and sub-clonal variants. In the corpus it is used as an orthogonal validation and deep-coverage method alongside WGS/WES PMID:25730765.




PMID:25730765 — Andersson et al., applied targeted DNA sequencing in infant MLL-rearranged ALL as part of a multi-platform strategy combining WGS, WES, and RNA-seq.





Used to validate and extend findings from whole-genome and whole-exome sequencing in infant MLL-R ALL PMID:25730765.
Enables detection of sub-clonal kinase/PI3K/RAS pathway mutations that may be missed at lower depth PMID:25730765.





PMID:25730765

title: IgCaller
text:
Computational tool for calling structural variants and rearrangements at immunoglobulin loci from short-read sequencing.




PMID:35927489 — used in the CLL-map SV analysis to identify Ig-locus rearrangements; in 177 WGS cases, BCL2 translocations were identified predominantly in M-CLL (5/88 WGS, 5.7%) via aberrant V(D)J. SV analysis across the full cohort was extrapolated from WES + IgCaller PMID:35927489.





The SV landscape of the full CLL cohort relied on only 177 WGS samples plus WES/IgCaller-based extrapolation, a noted limitation of the study PMID:35927489.

title: MSK-IMPACT 341 (IMPACT341)
text:
Targeted (341 cancer genes) sequencing of various tumor types via MSK-IMPACT on Illumina HiSeq sequencers — canonical cBioPortal gene panel IMPACT341. The earliest of the MSK-IMPACT panel versions used in the corpus.




PMID:37078708 — used (alongside IMPACT410 / IMPACT468 depending on era) to profile 132 adults with nodal peripheral T-cell lymphoma at MSK 2015–2020; TP53 mutations and TP53/17p deletions were the only somatic aberrancies correlating with inferior PFS on CHOP-based induction PMID:37078708.
PMID:37591896 — one of the MSK-IMPACT panel versions used to profile 233 resected NSCLC brain metastases plus matched primary and extracranial samples PMID:37591896.





Targeted panel rather than WES/WGS — non-panel drivers and full mutational signatures are not assessed PMID:37078708.

title: Fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH)
text:
Cytogenetic technique using fluorescent DNA probes to detect specific genomic rearrangements, fusions, and copy-number changes on interphase or metaphase nuclei.




PMID:37315267 — used with custom BAC probes flanking FOXO1, PAX3, and PAX7 to detect FOXO1 fusions in extremity rhabdomyosarcoma; complemented Archer FusionPlex testing. 85% of cases were FOXO1 fusion-positive ARMS, 70% of which carried PAX3::FOXO1 PMID:37315267.





FISH and Archer FusionPlex were used together for fusion confirmation in the extremity RMS cohort PMID:37315267.





PMID:37315267

title: CLUMPS
text:
3-D protein-structure mutation clustering method used to nominate driver genes whose somatic mutations cluster in protein space rather than along the linear sequence.




PMID:35927489 — used in the CLL-map driver-discovery pipeline alongside recurrent sSNV/indel testing; nominated six additional CLL drivers (including MAP2K2, DIS3, DICER1) on top of the 82 from recurrent SNV/indels PMID:35927489.





Specific CLUMPS-nominated driver examples in CLL: MAP2K2 (3 mutations in the kinase domain, activating ERK signaling analogous to MAP2K1), DIS3 (2/4 sites at cancer hotspots D479/D488 in the catalytic domain), DICER1, and INO80 (mutations clustered in the DNA-binding domain) PMID:35927489.

title: Whole-exome sequencing (WES)
text:
Capture-based sequencing of the protein-coding portion of the genome (~1–2% of the genome), used in the corpus for driver discovery, mutational signature analysis, copy-number, and neoantigen prediction.




PMID:35927489 — 984 WES CLL samples within the integrated CLL-map cohort (WES/WGS n=1,074); supported the nomination of 82 putative CLL drivers from recurrent sSNV/indels and 6 additional CLUMPS-nominated drivers PMID:35927489.
PMID:36723991 — WES on 36 cHL cases complementing 25 WGS cases for the combined 61-patient driver-gene cohort, recovering 26 mutated driver genes/hotspots in classic Hodgkin lymphoma PMID:36723991.
PMID:36862133 — whole-exome recapture on 62 ovarian GCT tumor/normal pairs from 59 patients in the Make-an-IMPACT program; uncovered a near-haploid genomic profile in 17% of female germ cell tumors, mutually exclusive with 12p gain PMID:36862133.
PMID:37084736 — WES subcohort within the LUAD organotropism study supported APOBEC mutational signature analysis (SBS2/SBS13 18% in metastases vs 8% in primaries, p=0.012, particularly enriched in liver metastases) PMID:37084736.
PMID:37202560 — WES on AC-ICAM colon cancer cohort (n=348) for neoantigen prediction and immunoediting quantification, refining the prognostic value of the ICR signature PMID:37202560.





Several corpus papers explicitly note WES (vs targeted panels) is what enables full mutational-signature decomposition and immunoediting analysis PMID:37084736 PMID:37202560.

PMID:37084736 — WES subcohort within the LUAD organotropism study supported APOBEC mutational signature analysis (SBS2/SBS13 18% in metastases vs 8% in primaries, p=0.012, particularly enriched in liver metastases) PMID:37084736.
PMID:37202560 — WES on AC-ICAM colon cancer cohort (n=348) for neoantigen prediction and immunoediting quantification, refining the prognostic value of the ICR signature PMID:37202560.

title: Whole-exome sequencing (WES)
text: Several corpus papers explicitly note WES (vs targeted panels) is what enables full mutational-signature decomposition and immunoediting analysis PMID:37084736 PMID:37202560.

title: ctDx Lung
text:
Resolution Bioscience ctDx Lung targeted next-generation sequencing panel for plasma cell-free DNA in non-small-cell lung cancer PMID:36357680.




PMID:36357680 — used to profile plasma ctDNA from 1,127 advanced NSCLC patients across MSK and GenesisCare (Sydney); ctDNA detection rate was 64% (722/1,127), and ctDNA detection was independently associated with shorter OS (HR 2.05, 95% CI 1.74–2.42, P<0.001) PMID:36357680.





Operationally, only 37/1,919 (2%) ctDx Lung or MSK-ACCESS samples failed vs 13% of tissue sequencing attempts; turnaround was 11 days (IQR 9–14) for ctDx Lung/MSK-ACCESS vs 33 days for MSK-IMPACT PMID:36357680.
25% of patients had genomic alterations detected only in ctDNA (not in time-matched tissue), enriched for subclonal resistance drivers such as RICTOR and PIK3CA PMID:36357680.
ctDx Lung has a small genomic footprint, which limits TMB correlation analyses PMID:36357680.

title: TCR sequencing (TCR-seq)
text:
Targeted sequencing of T-cell receptor (typically TCRβ) CDR3 regions to characterize T-cell repertoire diversity, clonality, and tumor-enriched clones.




PMID:37202560 — deep TCRβ sequencing (immunoSEQ) on 348 AC-ICAM colon cancer tumor/normal pairs; the ICR signature captured the presence of clonally expanded, tumor-enriched T cell clones and outperformed CMS and MSI classification for prognosis PMID:37202560.





TCR-seq complements bulk RNA-seq, WES, and 16S microbiome profiling in the AC-ICAM multi-omics workflow PMID:37202560.

title: Illumina 450k methylation array
text:
Bead-based array measuring DNA methylation at ~450,000 CpG sites across the human genome, used in cBioPortal corpus papers for epitype/epigenomic characterization PMID:35927489.




PMID:35927489 — DNA methylation profiling of 490 CLL samples (part of n=999 methylation cohort, complemented by RRBS n=509) supporting the n-CLL/i-CLL/m-CLL epitype framework and the epiCMIT mitotic clock PMID:35927489.





DNA methylome variation in CLL was dominated by cell-of-origin and proliferative history rather than driver mutation status PMID:35927489.

title: Log-linear mixed-effects model
text:
Statistical longitudinal model fit to log-transformed tumor volume measurements over time to estimate continuous tumor volume growth rate (TVGR) and doubling time, accounting for within-patient correlation across repeated scans.




PMID:37910594 — fit to serial volumetric MRI measurements from 128 IDH-mutant Grade 2 gliomas to estimate %TVGR per 6 months of 10.46% (95% CI 9.11–11.83) and doubling time of 3.5 years for the entire cohort; log-linear fit (adjusted r²=0.9864) marginally outperformed linear (r²=0.9556). Molecular grade-high (CDKN2A/B homozygous deletion) tumors had TVGR 19.17% per 6 months vs 9.54% for grade-low (P<0.0001) PMID:37910594.





Assumes exponential growth; log-linearity verified empirically for IDH-mt LGG across the observation window PMID:37910594.





PMID:37910594

title: 16S rRNA gene sequencing
text:
Amplicon sequencing of the bacterial 16S rRNA gene used to characterize microbial community composition in tumor and matched normal tissue PMID:37202560.




PMID:37202560 — applied to 348 AC-ICAM treatment-naive primary colon cancer tumor/normal pairs, plus an additional 42 tumor-only samples (ICAM42), to derive a Ruminococcus bromii-driven microbiome signature combined with the ICR signature into the mICRoScore PMID:37202560.





In the AC-ICAM workflow, 16S sequencing was paired with tumor whole-genome sequencing for microbiome read mining and validation PMID:37202560.
Microbiome composition is sensitive to sample handling, diet, and geography, so the R. bromii signal requires external validation PMID:37202560.

title: Consensus Molecular Subtype (CMS) classifier
text:
Transcriptome-based classifier assigning colorectal tumors to one of four Consensus Molecular Subtypes (CMS1–4).




PMID:37202560 — used as a comparator in the AC-ICAM colon cancer atlas; the 20-gene ICR signature outperformed CMS and MSI classifications for prognosis and retained prognostic value within the otherwise heterogeneous CMS4/mesenchymal subtype PMID:37202560.





NK and T cell subset abundances were highest in ICR-high tumors across all CMS subtypes, indicating ICR captures immune signal orthogonal to CMS PMID:37202560.

title: Archer FusionPlex
text:
Targeted RNA-based anchored multiplex PCR sequencing assay used to detect gene fusions, including partner-agnostic fusion calling.




PMID:37315267 — used to detect FOXO1 fusions (PAX3::FOXO1, PAX7::FOXO1) in extremity rhabdomyosarcoma, complementing FISH with custom BAC probes flanking FOXO1, PAX3, and PAX7 PMID:37315267.





85% of extremity ARMS cases in the cohort were FOXO1 fusion-positive; PAX3::FOXO1 had worse prognosis than PAX7::FOXO1 (HR 3.45, P=.016) PMID:37315267.

text:

title: erdafitinib
text:
Oral pan-FGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor targeting FGFR1/FGFR2/FGFR3/FGFR4; FDA-approved for FGFR2/3-altered metastatic urothelial carcinoma (PMID:37682528).




Real-world MSK cohort of 32 metastatic urothelial carcinoma patients treated 8/2019–7/2022: ORR 40% (12/30 evaluable), median PFS 2.8 months (95% CI 2.6–5.4), median OS 6.6 months (95% CI 4.6–17.3) — activity modest relative to BLC2001 (PMID:37682528).
Tolerability-limited: dose reductions in 38% (12/32), interruptions in 50% (16/32); TEAEs included hyperphosphatemia 84%, fatigue 59%, mucositis 47% (grade >=3 16%), PPE 38% (grade >=3 9%), dysgeusia 31% (PMID:37682528).
Archival primary vs metastatic FGFR3 status discordant in 26% (7/27) of paired cases, arguing for metastatic-site or cfDNA profiling to guide erdafitinib selection (PMID:37682528).
Baseline TP53 co-alteration associated with lower ORR (22%, 2/9) and enriched among primary progressors (PMID:37682528).





Acquired second-site FGFR3 kinase-domain mutations detected in serial cfDNA: V553M and N540S (plus K650M, R669G); in silico modeling supports V553M as a gatekeeper allele allosterically blocking erdafitinib binding (PMID:37682528).
Acquired TP53 mutations in 5/27 on-treatment cfDNA series as putative resistance driver (PMID:37682528).
Acquired activating AKT1 mutation in 1/27 as putative bypass resistance (PMID:37682528).

title: erdafitinib
text: Oral pan-FGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor targeting FGFR1/FGFR2/FGFR3/FGFR4; FDA-approved for FGFR2/3-altered metastatic urothelial carcinoma (PMID:37682528).

title: erdafitinib
text:
Real-world MSK cohort of 32 metastatic urothelial carcinoma patients treated 8/2019–7/2022: ORR 40% (12/30 evaluable), median PFS 2.8 months (95% CI 2.6–5.4), median OS 6.6 months (95% CI 4.6–17.3) — activity modest relative to BLC2001 (PMID:37682528).
Tolerability-limited: dose reductions in 38% (12/32), interruptions in 50% (16/32); TEAEs included hyperphosphatemia 84%, fatigue 59%, mucositis 47% (grade >=3 16%), PPE 38% (grade >=3 9%), dysgeusia 31% (PMID:37682528).
Archival primary vs metastatic FGFR3 status discordant in 26% (7/27) of paired cases, arguing for metastatic-site or cfDNA profiling to guide erdafitinib selection (PMID:37682528).
Baseline TP53 co-alteration associated with lower ORR (22%, 2/9) and enriched among primary progressors (PMID:37682528).

title: erdafitinib
text:
Acquired second-site FGFR3 kinase-domain mutations detected in serial cfDNA: V553M and N540S (plus K650M, R669G); in silico modeling supports V553M as a gatekeeper allele allosterically blocking erdafitinib binding (PMID:37682528).
Acquired TP53 mutations in 5/27 on-treatment cfDNA series as putative resistance driver (PMID:37682528).
Acquired activating AKT1 mutation in 1/27 as putative bypass resistance (PMID:37682528).

title: enfortumab-vedotin
text:
Nectin-4-directed antibody-drug conjugate delivering the microtubule disruptor MMAE; cited as an alternative systemic option in metastatic urothelial carcinoma (PMID:37682528).




Referenced as an alternative line of therapy for metastatic urothelial carcinoma in the context of the modest real-world activity (ORR 40%, PFS 2.8 mo) and tolerability burden of erdafitinib in FGFR3-altered disease (PMID:37682528).





Not characterized in the corpus.

title: etoposide
text:
Podophyllotoxin-derivative topoisomerase II inhibitor; included in CHOEP/EPOCH variants of CHOP-based lymphoma therapy.




45% of the 132 MSK nodal PTCL cohort received CHOEP/EPOCH (cyclophosphamide/doxorubicin/vincristine/prednisone plus etoposide); TP53 mutation status predicted markedly inferior PFS on these etoposide-containing regimens (HR 3.1, P=.005) PMID:37078708.





TP53 mutations and 17p deletions correlate with inferior PFS on CHOP/CHOEP-based induction PMID:37078708.

title: fludarabine
text:
Purine nucleoside analog; historical backbone of CLL chemoimmunotherapy. RRM1 (ribonucleotide reductase) is a direct target.




In the 1,148-patient CLL map, RRM1 — the molecular target of fludarabine — was nominated as a novel U-CLL-specific driver PMID:35927489.
In the contemporary era of ibrutinib/venetoclax (replacing fludarabine-based chemoimmunotherapy), isolated TP53 mutation without 17p deletion was no longer associated with adverse outcome in U-CLL PMID:35927489.





RRM1 alterations in U-CLL may alter fludarabine sensitivity PMID:35927489.

title: osimertinib
text:
Third-generation EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor active against T790M and sensitizing mutations, with CNS penetration.




Non-canonical EGFR mutations in NSCLC brain metastases may identify patients at elevated risk for leptomeningeal disease and partial resistance to osimertinib, with persistence across serial tissue/CSF samples despite maximal EGFR-directed and local therapy PMID:37591896.
One vignette showed emergence then clearance of EGFR T790M under osimertinib while ongoing L861Q/G719S drivers persisted PMID:37591896.
LMD patients had more EGFR alterations than non-progressors (45% vs 21%, p=0.044) PMID:37591896.





Non-canonical EGFR drivers (L861Q, G719A/S, A755G, N771_H773dup) persist under osimertinib and associate with leptomeningeal tropism PMID:37591896.
T790M can emerge and then clear under osimertinib while uncommon primary drivers remain PMID:37591896.





LUAD, NSCLC

title: KMT2C
text:
KMT2C (MLL3) is a histone H3K4 methyltransferase and a recurrent epigenetic-regulator driver in lymphoid malignancies.




Epigenetic regulator enriched in M-CLL, primarily via 7q36.1b loss (2.5%) PMID:35927489.





CLLSLL — M-CLL-enriched driver reflecting epigenetic pathway convergence in the IGHV-mutated subtype PMID:35927489.





Shows pathway convergence with KMT2D in M-CLL PMID:35927489.





None reported.

title: TP53
text:
TP53 is the canonical tumor suppressor and the most broadly cited gene in the cbio-kb corpus, recurrently altered across CLL, appendiceal adenocarcinoma, Hodgkin lymphoma, peripheral T-cell lymphoma, and lung adenocarcinoma with disease-specific prognostic implications.




TP53 was mutated in 9.1% of CLL patients and co-occurs with 17p deletion; TP53 mutation without 17p deletion was not associated with adverse outcome in U-CLL in the ibrutinib/venetoclax era PMID:35927489.
Oncogenic TP53 mutations were observed in a minority of female germ cell tumors profiled via Make-an-IMPACT, mostly clonal by WES PMID:36862133.
Detection of pathogenic TP53 alterations in ctDNA (vs tissue only) was associated with worse prognosis in advanced NSCLC PMID:36357680.
TP53 was mutated in 8% of classic Hodgkin lymphoma cases PMID:36723991.
TP53 mutations were observed in 16% (21/132) of nodal PTCL; 15/21 (71%) lacked concurrent TP53/17p deletion while 6/21 (29%) were co-deleted PMID:37078708.
TP53-mut predominant mucinous appendiceal adenocarcinoma tumors were highly aneuploid, invasive, and carried the worst prognosis; aneuploidy was an independent predictor of poor OS (P=0.001) PMID:36493333.
Inactivation of TP53 (together with SMARCA4 and CDKN2A) correlated with site-specific shorter time to metastasis in LUAD PMID:37084736.
TP53 alterations were enriched in LUAD primary tumors from patients who later developed brain metastasis vs non-metastatic primaries; TP53 was commonly shared between BM and PT/EM and often truncal PMID:37591896.
TP53 mutations were observed in 94% of 1p19q intact IDH-mutant astrocytomas in an MSKCC active-surveillance low-grade glioma cohort (MSK-IMPACT subcohort n=73) PMID:37910594.
TP53 was included in LUAD pathway/metastasis analyses in the MSK-CHORD clinicogenomic dataset (n=24,950) PMID:39506116.
In SDH-deficient GISTs (n=26), TP53 mutations portended worse recurrence-free and disease-free survival PMID:37477937.
TP53 clonal hematopoiesis VAFs consistently expanded at onset of subsequent hematologic malignancy in 26 MSK patients with paired post-diagnosis sequencing, consistent with preleukemic selection under therapy PMID:38147626.
In FGFR3-altered urothelial carcinoma, TP53 is inversely associated with FGFR3 alterations; baseline TP53 co-mutation linked to lower erdafitinib ORR (22% vs 40%) and enrichment among primary progressors (3/5); acquired TP53 mutations identified in 5/27 serial cfDNA cases as putative erdafitinib resistance drivers PMID:37682528.





CLLSLL — 9.1% mutated; 17p co-deletion modifies prognostic impact PMID:35927489.
APAD — defines the aneuploid, worst-prognosis MAAP molecular subtype PMID:36493333.
CHL — 8% mutated PMID:36723991.
PTCL — 16% mutated; inferior PFS (HR 3.1, P=0.005); median PFS 4.5 vs 10.5 months PMID:37078708.
LUAD / NSCLC — metastasis-associated driver, ctDNA-detected pathogenic alterations are independently prognostic PMID:37084736 PMID:36357680 PMID:37591896.
LCH / germ cell tumors — recurrently observed in rare-cancer profiling PMID:36862133.
BLCA / UTUC — inversely associated with FGFR3 alterations; co-mutation marks poor erdafitinib response PMID:37682528.





Significantly co-occurs with 17p deletion in CLL PMID:35927489.
Co-altered with EGFR/KEAP1/STK11 in ever-metastatic LUAD primaries (q<0.001) PMID:37084736.
Mutually exclusive with GNAS-mut and RAS-mut predominant molecular lineages of mucinous appendiceal adenocarcinoma PMID:36493333.





In the ibrutinib/venetoclax era, isolated TP53 mutation (without 17p loss) does not confer adverse prognosis in U-CLL PMID:35927489.
TP53 mutation status in PTCL identifies patients with markedly inferior PFS on CHOP-based induction and may warrant alternative induction/consolidation strategies PMID:37078708.





Discordance between PFS and OS effect of TP53 in PTCL is unexplained (possible salvage-therapy confounding) PMID:37078708.
Mechanistic basis of TP53-mut-associated aneuploidy in appendiceal tumors remains to be determined PMID:36493333.

title: DICER1
text:
DICER1 encodes the endoribonuclease essential for miRNA biogenesis; rare somatic and germline mutations generate distinct cancer predispositions and hotspot-driven oncogenic events.




Nominated as a novel CLL driver via 3-D clustering with CLUMPS in the 1,148-patient integrated CLL map PMID:35927489.
Also disrupted by the recurrent ~37-Mb chromosome 14 deletion observed in U-CLL (4/87 WGS, 4.6%), which arises via class-switch recombination and simultaneously disrupts ZFP36L1 and TRAF3 PMID:35927489.





CLL — DICER1 is a novel CLUMPS-nominated driver with both point-mutation and structural-variant modes in the U-CLL subtype PMID:35927489.





DICER1 is co-disrupted with ZFP36L1 and TRAF3 by the recurrent U-CLL chr14 class-switch-recombination deletion PMID:35927489.





No DICER1-directed therapy is reported in the corpus PMID:35927489.





Functional validation of DICER1 as a CLL driver, one of the 109 novel drivers in the CLL map, is deferred to future work PMID:35927489.

title: TNFAIP3
text:
TNFAIP3 (A20) is a negative regulator of NF-κB signaling and a recurrent driver in classic Hodgkin lymphoma.




TNFAIP3 was mutated in 36% of classic Hodgkin lymphoma cases — the second most common driver after SOCS1 — and is an NF-κB pathway driver PMID:36723991.





CHL — 36% mutated; NF-κB pathway driver PMID:36723991.





Part of the NF-κB driver set in cHL alongside NFKBIE PMID:36723991.





No direct targeted therapy reported in the corpus.





None specific to TNFAIP3 in the corpus.

title: STAT3
text:
STAT3 is a JAK/STAT pathway transcription factor recurrently altered in peripheral T-cell lymphoma.




STAT3 was among recurrently altered genes profiled in subset analyses of nodal peripheral T-cell lymphoma treated with CHOP-based chemotherapy PMID:37078708.





PTCL — recurrently altered in subset analyses PMID:37078708.

Independent prognostic effect not established in the PTCL cohort PMID:37078708.

title: PDGFRA
text:
PDGFRA encodes platelet-derived growth factor receptor alpha; activating mutations and amplifications drive a subset of GIST and gliomas.




Focal PDGFRA amplifications (along with CDK4 and MYCN) were used to define “molecular grade-intermediate” in 1p19q intact IDH-mutant astrocytomas in a 128-patient MSKCC active-surveillance cohort PMID:37910594.
PDGFRA is a co-primary GIST driver; KIT/PDGFRA wild-type cases with SDHB IHC loss were classified as SDH-deficient in the MSK GIST genomic risk model cohort PMID:37477937.





AST / DIFG — focal amplification marks intermediate molecular grade in IDH-mutant astrocytoma PMID:37910594.
GIST — co-primary driver alongside KIT PMID:37477937.





Mutually exclusive with KIT mutation in GIST PMID:37477937.

title: ITPKB
text:
ITPKB encodes inositol-trisphosphate 3-kinase B. It is a recurrent driver in classic Hodgkin lymphoma HRS cells.




Mutated in 28% of cHL cases; tended to carry more than one nonsynonymous mutation per case PMID:36723991.
Elevated noncoding:coding mutation ratio consistent with AID off-target activity PMID:36723991.





CHL — recurrent driver in FACS-purified HRS cells across WGS+WES cohort (n=61) PMID:36723991.





Grouped with BCL7A, SOCS1, TMSB4X, and IL4R as genes with elevated noncoding:coding mutation ratios PMID:36723991.





None reported.





Functional consequence of multi-hit and noncoding ITPKB lesions in cHL remains to be characterized PMID:36723991.

title: RRM1
text:
RRM1 encodes the large subunit of ribonucleotide reductase and is a target of the nucleoside analog fludarabine. In the corpus it is nominated as a novel U-CLL driver.




Novel U-CLL-specific driver in the 1,148-patient CLL map; target of fludarabine PMID:35927489.





Chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLLSLL) — novel U-CLL driver PMID:35927489.





Enriched in IGHV-unmutated (U-CLL) subtype PMID:35927489.





RRM1 is the molecular target of fludarabine; mutations potentially relevant to chemoresistance PMID:35927489.





Functional validation of RRM1 as a CLL driver and its interaction with fludarabine sensitivity is deferred to future work PMID:35927489.

title: TBX21
text:
TBX21 (T-bet) is a master Th1 transcription factor included in the 20-gene ICR immune signature.




TBX21 is a component of the 20-gene Immunologic Constant of Rejection (ICR) panel (Th1 signaling genes) used to stratify colon cancer immune subtypes PMID:37202560.





COAD — ICR panel component contributing to ICR-high vs ICR-low prognostic stratification PMID:37202560.





Co-expressed with other ICR Th1 genes (IFNG, CD8A, STAT1, IRF1) as a module PMID:37202560.





No direct targeted therapy reported in the corpus; ICR module may have predictive utility for immunotherapy PMID:37202560.





None specific to TBX21 in the corpus.

title: FOXP3
text:
FOXP3 is the master transcription factor of regulatory T cells (Tregs), and is used as a marker of immunoregulatory/counter-activation activity in tumor immune signatures.




Component of the 20-gene Immunologic Constant of Rejection (ICR) signature as an immunoregulatory counter-activation marker in colon cancer PMID:37202560.





COAD — expression measured as part of the ICR signature in the AC-ICAM cohort, where ICR-high tumors carried better OS PMID:37202560.





Co-expressed with CD274, CTLA4, IDO1, PDCD1 in the immunoregulatory arm of ICR PMID:37202560.





Not reported as a direct target in the corpus; relevant as an ICR component for prognostic stratification PMID:37202560.





None flagged in the corpus.

title: KEAP1
text:
KEAP1 is a substrate adaptor for NRF2 ubiquitination; loss of function drives NRF2 pathway activation. In LUAD it is a recognized poor-prognosis co-mutation partner.




One of ten genes differentially altered between ever- and never-metastatic LUAD primary tumors PMID:37084736.
Mutually exclusive with EGFR alterations PMID:37084736.





LUAD — enriched in ever-metastatic primaries (MSK cohort, n=2,532) PMID:37084736.





TP53/EGFR co-alteration with KEAP1 (q<0.001) was significant only in ever-metastatic primaries PMID:37084736.
Mutually exclusive with EGFR alterations PMID:37084736.
KEAP1 was included in LUAD pathway/metastasis analyses in MSK-CHORD (n=24,950) PMID:39506116.





Co-alteration patterns including KEAP1 may inform site-specific metastatic risk stratification in LUAD PMID:37084736.





None noted in the corpus.

title: CXCL9
text:
CXCL9 is an IFN-gamma-induced CXCR3 ligand that directs CD8 T cell and NK cell recruitment into inflamed and tumor tissues.




Included in the 20-gene Immunologic Constant of Rejection (ICR) signature as a CXCR3/CCR5 chemokine ligand, alongside CCL5 and CXCL10, applied to the 348-patient AC-ICAM colon cancer cohort PMID:37202560.





Colon adenocarcinoma — CXCL9 contributes to ICR-based prognostic clustering independent of CMS and MSI PMID:37202560.





Co-expressed with the ICR Th1/chemokine/effector module in ICR-high colon tumors PMID:37202560.





No direct CXCL9-targeted therapy is evaluated; it contributes to the prognostic ICR module PMID:37202560.





Independent contribution of CXCL9 vs the ICR module is not dissected PMID:37202560.

title: MAP2K2

title: CDKN2A
text:
CDKN2A encodes the p16^INK4a and p14^ARF tumor suppressors that restrain CDK4/6-RB and MDM2-p53 axes; deep deletions are recurrent drivers across lymphoid and solid tumors and carry prognostic weight.




Homozygous/heterozygous CDKN2A deletions in 7% (9/132) of nodal peripheral T-cell lymphomas on MSK-IMPACT; strong independent predictor of inferior OS (HR 12.1; 95% CI 2.8–52.0; P<.001) with median OS 17.6 vs 56.7 months; 3/4 MEITL cases carried CDKN2A aberrancies PMID:37078708.
CDKN2A inactivation correlated with site-specific shorter time to metastasis in the 2,532-specimen LUAD organotropism cohort, with inactivation more frequent in CNS and liver metastases than in matched primary tumors PMID:37084736.
CDKN2A was among the recurrent events in extremity alveolar RMS (8%–17% frequency); CDKN2A deletions were mutually exclusive with CDK4 amplification, enriched in acral and high-risk lesions, and correlated with poor OS (P=.02) PMID:37315267.
CDKN2A/CDKN2B alterations were enriched in NSCLC brain metastases compared with primary tumors (34% BM vs 13% PT; p=0.003, q=0.04), with cell-cycle pathway alterations 56% BM vs 32% PT (p=0.004, q=0.041) driven by CDKN2A/B; in the LUAD subset the enrichment persisted (31% BM vs 18% PT; p=0.004) PMID:37591896.
Frequently co-altered with FGFR3 in urothelial carcinoma (alongside CDKN2B and KDM6A) in the MSK bladder_msk_2023 cohort PMID:37682528.
Homozygous CDKN2A/B deletion (“molecular grade-high”) drove ~2x faster tumor-volume growth (19.17% per 6 months vs 9.54%) in IDH-mutant WHO Grade 2 low-grade glioma on active surveillance, supporting WHO 2021 Grade 4 upgrade PMID:37910594.
High small bowel GIST risk class in an elastic-net Cox genomic risk model was defined in part by CDKN2A alterations (along with RB1 and MAX/MGA/MYC) PMID:37477937.
CDKN2A loss characterized “biliary-class” intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (along with KRAS and SMAD4) in the MSK hidden-genome classifier cohort, associated with markedly worse OS PMID:38864854.





Peripheral T-cell lymphoma (notably MEITL) — CDKN2A deletion is a rare but very high-risk OS marker PMID:37078708.
LUAD — CDKN2A loss shortens time to metastasis and is enriched in CNS/liver mets PMID:37084736.
NSCLC brain metastasis — CDKN2A/B deletions are enriched vs primaries, marking cell-cycle dysregulation as near-obligate for BM development PMID:37591896.
Extremity alveolar RMS — CDKN2A deletion is recurrent and adverse PMID:37315267.





CDKN2A deletion is mutually exclusive with CDK4 amplification in extremity ARMS PMID:37315267.
CDKN2A/CDKN2B co-altered as a pair in NSCLC brain metastases, together driving cell-cycle pathway enrichment PMID:37591896.





CDKN2A/B loss in NSCLC BM is proposed as rationale for CDK4/6-directed strategies in CNS-tropic disease, though not tested in the corpus PMID:37591896.
Extremity RMS authors similarly raise CDK4/6 inhibition as a candidate strategy for CDKN2A-deleted tumors PMID:37315267.





Whether CDK4/6 inhibition improves outcomes in CDKN2A-deleted PTCL, LUAD BM, or extremity RMS is untested PMID:37078708 PMID:37591896 PMID:37315267.

title: CDKN2A
text: CDKN2A encodes the p16^INK4a and p14^ARF tumor suppressors that restrain CDK4/6-RB and MDM2-p53 axes; deep deletions are recurrent drivers across lymphoid and solid tumors and carry prognostic weight.

title: CDKN2A
text:
Homozygous/heterozygous CDKN2A deletions in 7% (9/132) of nodal peripheral T-cell lymphomas on MSK-IMPACT; strong independent predictor of inferior OS (HR 12.1; 95% CI 2.8–52.0; P<.001) with median OS 17.6 vs 56.7 months; 3/4 MEITL cases carried CDKN2A aberrancies PMID:37078708.
CDKN2A inactivation correlated with site-specific shorter time to metastasis in the 2,532-specimen LUAD organotropism cohort, with inactivation more frequent in CNS and liver metastases than in matched primary tumors PMID:37084736.
CDKN2A was among the recurrent events in extremity alveolar RMS (8%–17% frequency); CDKN2A deletions were mutually exclusive with CDK4 amplification, enriched in acral and high-risk lesions, and correlated with poor OS (P=.02) PMID:37315267.
CDKN2A/CDKN2B alterations were enriched in NSCLC brain metastases compared with primary tumors (34% BM vs 13% PT; p=0.003, q=0.04), with cell-cycle pathway alterations 56% BM vs 32% PT (p=0.004, q=0.041) driven by CDKN2A/B; in the LUAD subset the enrichment persisted (31% BM vs 18% PT; p=0.004) PMID:37591896.

High small bowel GIST risk class in an elastic-net Cox genomic risk model was defined in part by CDKN2A alterations (along with RB1 and MAX/MGA/MYC) PMID:37477937.
CDKN2A loss characterized “biliary-class” intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (along with KRAS and SMAD4) in the MSK hidden-genome classifier cohort, associated with markedly worse OS PMID:38864854.

title: DNMT3A
text:
DNMT3A encodes a de novo DNA methyltransferase and is a recurrently mutated epigenetic regulator across T-cell lymphomas and myeloid malignancies.




Mutated in 19% (25/132) of nodal peripheral T-cell lymphomas profiled on MSK-IMPACT, making it the third-most-frequently altered gene after TET2 and RHOA PMID:37078708.
DNMT3A is the most prevalent clonal hematopoiesis driver in MSK patients with nonhematologic cancer; in 26 patients who developed a hematologic malignancy, DNMT3A CH VAFs largely decreased at disease onset (in contrast to JAK2/TP53 expansion) PMID:38147626.





PTCL / AITL — 19% mutation frequency in the MSK nodal PTCL cohort PMID:37078708.





Co-occurs within the TET2/RHOA/IDH2-altered TFH-phenotype PTCL landscape PMID:37078708.





No independent prognostic effect reported in the PTCL cohort treated with CHOP-based chemotherapy PMID:37078708.





None flagged in the corpus.

title: STK11
text:
STK11 (LKB1) is a tumor suppressor kinase recurrently inactivated in lung adenocarcinoma and associated with aggressive, metastatic disease.




TP53/EGFR co-alteration with STK11 (q<0.001) was significant only in ever-metastatic LUAD primaries PMID:37084736.
SMARCA4, STK11, KEAP1, and KRAS alterations were mutually exclusive with EGFR alterations in LUAD PMID:37084736.
STK11 alterations were present in 22% of LUAD brain metastases vs 0% in squamous-cell carcinoma brain metastases (p=0.01) PMID:37591896.
STK11 was included in LUAD pathway/metastasis analyses in MSK-CHORD (n=24,950) PMID:39506116.





LUAD — co-altered with TP53/EGFR in ever-metastatic primaries PMID:37084736; enriched in LUAD vs LUSC brain metastases PMID:37591896.





Co-occurs with TP53/EGFR+KEAP1 in ever-metastatic LUAD PMID:37084736.
Mutually exclusive with EGFR alterations in LUAD PMID:37084736.





No direct targeted therapy reported in the corpus.





Mechanism by which STK11 loss contributes to specific metastatic tropism is not addressed in the corpus.

title: RHOA
text:
RHOA is a small GTPase. In peripheral T-cell lymphoma, hotspot RHOA G17V mutations are a hallmark of angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma (AITL).




Mutated in 30% (40/132) of adults with nodal PTCL treated with curative-intent CHOP-based chemotherapy at MSK PMID:37078708.
In AITL, 88% of RHOA mutations were the canonical G17V variant PMID:37078708.





Peripheral T-cell lymphoma (PTCL, AITL) — 30% overall, G17V hotspot in AITL PMID:37078708.





Commonly co-mutates with TET2 and DNMT3A in PTCL PMID:37078708.





No independent prognostic effect on PFS/OS reported; no direct targeted therapy association in the corpus PMID:37078708.





None specific to RHOA in this cohort.





PMID:37078708

title: CCL5
text:
CCL5 (RANTES) is a CCR5 ligand chemokine that recruits effector T cells and is a core component of tumor immune infiltration signatures.




CCL5 is one of the 20 genes in the Immunologic Constant of Rejection (ICR) signature applied to the 348-patient Sidra-LUMC AC-ICAM colon cancer cohort, grouped with the CXCR3/CCR5 chemokine ligands CXCL9 and CXCL10 PMID:37202560.





Colon adenocarcinoma — CCL5 contributes to ICR-based consensus clustering that segregates ICR-high, -medium, and -low tumors with distinct overall survival PMID:37202560.





Co-expressed with the other ICR Th1/cytotoxic module genes (IFNG, CD8A, CD8B, CXCL9, CXCL10, GZMA/B, PRF1) in AC-ICAM PMID:37202560.





No direct CCL5-targeting therapy is evaluated; the ICR module as a whole is proposed as a prognostic and potentially predictive biomarker PMID:37202560.





The paper treats CCL5 as a module component rather than a standalone biomarker; independent contribution is not dissected PMID:37202560.





PMID:37202560

title: RFX7
text:
RFX7 is a regulatory factor X family transcription factor. In the corpus it is nominated as a novel U-CLL driver with adverse prognostic impact.




Novel U-CLL driver associated with poor FFS/OS in the 1,148-patient CLL map PMID:35927489.





Chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLLSLL) — novel U-CLL-enriched driver PMID:35927489.





Enriched in IGHV-unmutated (U-CLL) subtype PMID:35927489.





No direct therapeutic link reported in the corpus.





Functional validation of RFX7 as a CLL driver is deferred to future work PMID:35927489.

title: IL12B
text:
IL12B encodes the p40 subunit shared by IL-12 and IL-23, key cytokines driving Th1 differentiation and cytotoxic immunity.




Th1-signaling component of the 20-gene ICR signature used in the AC-ICAM colon cancer atlas PMID:37202560.





COAD — ICR Th1 component in the AC-ICAM cohort PMID:37202560.





Co-expressed with IFNG, TBX21, CD8A, CD8B, STAT1, IRF1 in the ICR Th1 arm PMID:37202560.

title: CDKN1A
text:
CDKN1A encodes p21, a cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor and p53 effector that enforces G1/S cell-cycle arrest. It is recurrently altered in urothelial carcinoma.




CDKN1A was among the genes recurrently altered in FGFR3-altered urothelial carcinoma in the MSK bladder_msk_2023 cohort PMID:37682528.





BLCA / UTUC — recurrent alteration in FGFR3-altered UC PMID:37682528.





Co-occurs with FGFR3 alterations in urothelial carcinoma PMID:37682528.





Not directly linked to therapy response in the corpus.





Prognostic or predictive significance of CDKN1A alterations in FGFR3-altered UC remains undefined PMID:37682528.

title: NFKBIE
text:
NFKBIE encodes IkB-epsilon, an inhibitor of NF-kB signaling. In the corpus it appears as a recurrent low-frequency driver in CLL and as an NF-kB pathway driver in classic Hodgkin lymphoma.




Known lower-frequency CLL driver, reconfirmed as prognostic in U-CLL in the 1,148-patient CLL map PMID:35927489.
Mutated in 15% of classic Hodgkin lymphoma cases profiled by WGS/WES (n=61); NF-kB pathway driver PMID:36723991.





Chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLLSLL) — prognostic in U-CLL PMID:35927489.
Classic Hodgkin lymphoma (CHL) — 15% mutation frequency PMID:36723991.





Part of the NF-kB pathway driver set in cHL alongside TNFAIP3 PMID:36723991.





No direct therapeutic link reported in the corpus.





Mechanistic role relative to other NF-kB drivers across B-cell malignancies remains open.

title: PTPN1
text:
PTPN1 (PTP1B) is a tyrosine phosphatase and negative regulator of JAK/STAT signaling. In the corpus it is a classic Hodgkin lymphoma driver and one of the temporally earliest pathogenic events in cHL.




Mutated in 8% of classic Hodgkin lymphoma cases across 61 WGS/WES-profiled patients PMID:36723991.
Evolutionary timing analysis placed PTPN1 driver mutations (together with B2M, BCL7A, GNA13) before large chromosomal gains and whole-genome duplication, making it an early event in cHL pathogenesis PMID:36723991.





Classic Hodgkin lymphoma (CHL) — 8% mutation frequency; early driver PMID:36723991.





Grouped with B2M, BCL7A, GNA13 as early drivers preceding WGD PMID:36723991.





No direct therapeutic link reported in the corpus.





How early PTPN1 events in cHL relate to equivalent early events in DLBCL and other AID-driven B-cell lymphomas PMID:36723991.

title: NFKB1
text:
NFKB1 encodes a subunit of the NF-kB transcription factor complex. In the cbio-kb corpus it is nominated as a novel U-CLL driver linked to NF-kB signaling and adverse outcomes.




U-CLL-specific novel driver in the 1,148-patient CLL map; recurrent somatic mutations linked to NF-kB signaling and adverse FFS/OS PMID:35927489.





Chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLLSLL) — novel U-CLL driver; candidate addition to CLL risk models PMID:35927489.





Enriched in IGHV-unmutated (U-CLL) subtype relative to M-CLL PMID:35927489.





No direct therapeutic link reported in the corpus.





Functional validation as a CLL driver is deferred to future work PMID:35927489.

title: NOTCH1
text:
NOTCH1 is a canonical CLL driver. In the corpus it is one of the four cardinal CLL drivers in the 1,148-patient CLL map.




Mutated in 12.3% of CLL patients in the CLL map; a cardinal known CLL driver PMID:35927489.
Temporally intermediate in both M-CLL and U-CLL subtypes by PhylogicNDT ordering PMID:35927489.
Negatively regulated by ZFP36L1, which is disrupted by the recurrent 37-Mb chr14 deletion in U-CLL PMID:35927489.





Chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLLSLL) — 12.3% mutation frequency PMID:35927489.





Indirect pathway activation via ZFP36L1 loss in U-CLL PMID:35927489.





No direct therapeutic link reported in the corpus.





Relationship of NOTCH1 mutation status to expression clusters and epitype PMID:35927489.





PMID:35927489

title: ATM
text:
ATM is a core DNA damage response kinase and one of the cardinal drivers of chronic lymphocytic leukemia.




Mutated in 11.2% of patients in the 1,148-patient CLL map, making it one of the top four cardinal CLL drivers alongside SF3B1, NOTCH1, and TP53 PMID:35927489.





CLL — ATM is a cardinal known driver recovered as significantly mutated across the integrated CLL cohort PMID:35927489.





Not specifically reported in the corpus for ATM partners PMID:35927489.





No ATM-directed therapy is reported in the corpus for CLL PMID:35927489.





The CLL map does not resolve whether ATM mutation prognostic impact is preserved in the ibrutinib/venetoclax era PMID:35927489.

title: ATRX
text:
ATRX is a SWI/SNF-family chromatin remodeler whose loss enables the alternative lengthening of telomeres (ALT) phenotype and is a defining alteration of IDH-mutant astrocytomas.




ATRX mutations observed in 77% of 1p19q intact IDH-mutant astrocytomas in the MSK-IMPACT subcohort (n=73) of a low-grade glioma active-surveillance study PMID:37910594.





AST / DIFG — canonical co-alteration in IDH-mutant 1p19q intact astrocytoma PMID:37910594.





Co-occurs with TP53 in 1p19q intact IDH-mutant astrocytomas PMID:37910594.





Not directly therapeutically targeted in the corpus.

title: ALK
text:
ALK is a receptor tyrosine kinase recurrently activated by gene fusions in multiple cancer types, including histiocytic neoplasms where it provides a targetable driver alteration.




TFG-ALK fusion identified in one histiocytosis patient profiled on MSK-IMPACT with targeted RNA sequencing PMID:36862133.





Histiocytosis (LCH/ECD) — ALK fusions are a rare but actionable driver class within the broader MAPK/kinase-driven histiocytosis landscape PMID:36862133.





Within histiocytosis, ALK fusions co-classify with BRAF, MAP2K1, KRAS, CSF1R, and NTRK1 as the set of potentially actionable kinase-pathway drivers PMID:36862133.





ALK fusions in histiocytosis are candidates for matched targeted therapy through the Make-an-IMPACT direct-to-patient program, in which 17/18 histiocytosis patients who received genomically matched therapy had clinical benefit (mean duration 21.7 months) PMID:36862133.





Whether ALK-fusion histiocytosis responds durably to ALK inhibitors is not directly evaluated in the corpus PMID:36862133.





PMID:36862133

title: FGFR2
text:
FGFR2 is a receptor tyrosine kinase whose activating fusions and mutations are actionable with FGFR inhibitors in multiple tumor types, but in urothelial carcinoma it is vanishingly rare compared with FGFR3.




Only 1/1,507 urothelial tumors had a potentially actionable FGFR2 alteration (an FGFR2:MARVELD3 fusion of unclear significance) in the MSK bladder_msk_2023 cohort PMID:37682528.
FGFR2 is an actionable driver in intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma; the MSK hidden-genome classifier predicted overall survival in IHC independently of FGFR2 alteration status PMID:38864854.





BLCA / UTUC — actionable FGFR2 alterations are essentially absent PMID:37682528.





Not reported in the corpus.





In principle eligible for erdafitinib alongside FGFR3, but the near-absence of FGFR2 events in UC limits clinical impact in this disease PMID:37682528.





Clinical significance of the observed FGFR2:MARVELD3 fusion is unclear PMID:37682528.





PMID:37682528

title: FOXO1
text:
FOXO1 is a forkhead transcription factor whose PAX3/PAX7 fusions define alveolar rhabdomyosarcoma (ARMS).




FOXO1 fusion partner in 85% of extremity alveolar rhabdomyosarcomas (detected by Archer FusionPlex or FISH with custom BAC probes) PMID:37315267.
PAX3::FOXO1 present in 70% of ARMS and associated with worse prognosis and older age than PAX7::FOXO1 (HR 3.45, P=.016) PMID:37315267.





ARMS / RMS — defining fusion event in extremity ARMS PMID:37315267.





Co-occurs with MED12 alterations, CDK4 amplifications, and CDKN2A deletions in ARMS PMID:37315267.





Fusion-partner subtype (PAX3 vs PAX7) carries independent prognostic weight and may warrant fusion-aware risk stratification PMID:37315267.





None flagged in the corpus.





PMID:37315267

title: PAX7
text:
PAX7 is a paired-box transcription factor. PAX7::FOXO1 fusions define a more favorable prognostic subset of alveolar rhabdomyosarcoma relative to PAX3::FOXO1.




PAX7::FOXO1 fusion present in a minority of extremity ARMS cases in the 61-patient MSK cohort; detected by Archer FusionPlex or FISH PMID:37315267.
PAX7::FOXO1 ARMS carries more favorable prognosis relative to PAX3::FOXO1 (HR 3.45 favoring PAX7, P=.016) PMID:37315267.





Alveolar rhabdomyosarcoma (ARMS) — favorable fusion partner PMID:37315267.





Mutually exclusive with PAX3::FOXO1 fusions in ARMS PMID:37315267.





Supports fusion-partner-aware risk stratification PMID:37315267.





Biological basis for prognostic divergence between PAX3 and PAX7 fusion subtypes PMID:37315267.

title: TMSB4X
text:
TMSB4X is recurrently mutated in classic Hodgkin lymphoma via AID off-target activity.




TMSB4X showed an elevated noncoding:coding mutation ratio in classic Hodgkin lymphoma HRS cells, consistent with AID off-target activity PMID:36723991.





CHL — AID off-target mutation hotspot PMID:36723991.





Co-occurs among AID-target genes (BCL7A, SOCS1, IL4R, ITPKB) PMID:36723991.





No direct targeted therapy reported in the corpus.





Functional impact of noncoding AID-driven TMSB4X mutations on cHL biology is not resolved PMID:36723991.

title: TACC3
text:
TACC3 is a centrosomal/spindle protein best known in cancer genomics as the canonical 3’ fusion partner of FGFR3, producing oncogenic FGFR3-TACC3 fusions.




FGFR3-TACC3 fusions were observed among oncogenic FGFR3 alterations in urothelial carcinoma; FGFR3-fusion tumors had lower TMB than FGFR3-mutant tumors (median 5 vs 9 mut/Mb, p=0.0006) PMID:37682528.





BLCA / UTUC — FGFR3-TACC3 fusions contribute to the actionable FGFR3-altered population PMID:37682528.





Obligate partner of FGFR3 in FGFR3-TACC3 fusions PMID:37682528.





FGFR3-TACC3-fusion urothelial tumors are eligible for erdafitinib PMID:37682528.





Whether FGFR3-fusion tumors respond differently to erdafitinib than FGFR3-mutant tumors is not directly resolved in the corpus PMID:37682528.

title: JAK2
text:
JAK2 encodes a non-receptor tyrosine kinase central to cytokine signaling; the V617F gain-of-function mutation defines myeloproliferative neoplasms and is also a high-risk clonal hematopoiesis driver.




JAK2 was identified as a high-risk clonal hematopoiesis (CH) genotype conferring particularly high risk of subsequent hematologic malignancy in 42,714 MSK patients with nonhematologic cancer profiled by MSK-IMPACT matched-normal sequencing PMID:38147626.
In 26 patients who developed a hematologic malignancy with paired post-diagnosis sequencing, JAK2 CH VAFs consistently expanded at disease onset (in contrast to DNMT3A and silent CH VAFs which mostly decreased) PMID:38147626.





Clonal hematopoiesis in patients with nonhematologic cancer — JAK2-mutant CH anticipates progression to hematologic malignancy PMID:38147626.





Not specifically reported in the corpus.





JAK2-mutant CH may warrant closer hematologic surveillance in solid-tumor patients receiving genotoxic therapy PMID:38147626.





The degree to which specific prior therapies drive expansion of JAK2 clones was not resolved PMID:38147626.





PMID:38147626

title: ERBB2
text:
ERBB2 (HER2) is a receptor tyrosine kinase whose amplification and activating mutations drive multiple solid tumors and define HER2-directed therapy eligibility.




ERBB2 was among the genes altered less often in LUAD metastases than in matched primaries PMID:37084736.
ERBB2 amplification was identified in one female ovarian germ cell tumor treated with trastuzumab, without durable response PMID:36862133.
In urothelial carcinoma, ERBB2 was inversely associated with FGFR3 alterations overall but co-altered in 5% (19/414) of FGFR3-altered tumors, ranging from 2.5% in NMIBC to 11% in MIBC PMID:37682528.





LUAD — depleted in metastases vs primaries PMID:37084736.
OGCT — amplification in one patient in the Make-an-IMPACT rare-cancer cohort PMID:36862133.
BLCA / UTUC — co-altered in 11% of FGFR3-altered MIBC PMID:37682528.





Not reported in the corpus.





trastuzumab treatment of an ERBB2-amplified ovarian GCT did not produce a durable response PMID:36862133.





None flagged in the corpus.

title: SETD2
text:
SETD2 is a histone H3K36 methyltransferase that maintains chromatin and transcriptional fidelity; loss-of-function mutations are recurrent across renal, lung, and other cancers.




Of 5,957 LUAD patients in MSK-CHORD, 204 (3%) had SETD2 driver mutations, which predicted longer overall survival and lower rates of CNS metastasis PMID:39506116.
SETD2 mutation was associated with longer time to next treatment or death following immune checkpoint blockade but not cytotoxic chemotherapy or molecularly targeted therapy; the immunotherapy association held among TMB-low (<10 mut/Mb) patients and replicated in two independent validation cohorts (DFCI and a commercial RWD cohort) PMID:39506116.
SETD2 mutation was associated with a small but statistically significant increase in TMB (Mann-Whitney P = 2x10^-9) PMID:39506116.





LUAD / NSCLC — uncommon (~3%) driver predictive of longer OS, lower CNS metastasis, and immune checkpoint blockade response PMID:39506116.





Positively associated with BRAF and ARID1A alterations and negatively associated with EGFR and MDM2 alterations and mucinous subtype in LUAD (q<0.05) PMID:39506116.





Proposed as an uncommon but promising biomarker of immune checkpoint blockade response in LUAD not explained by TMB, PD-L1, smoking status or histologic subtype; prospective validation suggested PMID:39506116.





Mechanism linking SETD2 loss to improved immunotherapy response in LUAD remains unresolved PMID:39506116.





PMID:39506116

title: SMAD4
text:
SMAD4 is a tumor suppressor central to TGF-beta signaling; loss-of-function mutations and deletions are recurrent in pancreatic, colorectal, and biliary tract cancers.




SMAD4 alterations characterized “biliary-class” intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (along with KRAS and CDKN2A loss) in the MSK hidden-genome classifier (n=527 IHC), associated with markedly worse OS than HCC-class IHC PMID:38864854.





IHCH — SMAD4 is enriched in biliary-class IHC and discriminative of EHC/GBC-like genetics PMID:38864854.





Co-occurs with KRAS and CDKN2A loss in biliary-class IHC PMID:38864854.





Not directly therapeutically targeted in the corpus, but SMAD4-positive biliary-class IHC has substantially worse prognosis informing trial stratification PMID:38864854.





None flagged.

title: GNA13
text:
GNA13 encodes a Gα13 heterotrimeric G-protein subunit and is a recurrently inactivated tumor suppressor in germinal-center-derived B-cell lymphomas.




Mutated in 26% of classic Hodgkin lymphoma (cHL) cases in a combined WGS+WES cohort (n=61) PMID:36723991.
Enriched in EBV-negative cHL cases PMID:36723991.
Timed as an early event preceding large chromosomal gains/WGD in cHL evolution PMID:36723991.





CHL — common, early driver mutation in HRS cells PMID:36723991.





Grouped among early drivers (B2M, BCL7A, GNA13, PTPN1) preceding WGD PMID:36723991.





Not directly evaluated in the corpus.





How temporally early cHL drivers (B2M, BCL7A, GNA13, PTPN1) relate to the equivalent early events in DLBCL and other AID-exposed B-cell lymphomas PMID:36723991.

title: CDK4
text:
CDK4 encodes a cyclin-dependent kinase central to G1/S cell-cycle progression; focal amplification deregulates the RB pathway and is therapeutically targeted by CDK4/6 inhibitors.




Focal CDK4 amplifications were among the most common genomic events in alveolar rhabdomyosarcoma (ARMS) in the 61-patient MSKCC extremity RMS cohort, with frequencies in the 8%–17% range alongside MED12 alterations and CDKN2A deletions PMID:37315267.
CDK4 amplifications and CDKN2A deletions were mutually exclusive, enriched in acral and high-risk lesions, and correlated with poor overall survival (P=.02) PMID:37315267.
Focal CDK4 amplifications (along with MYCN and PDGFRA) were used to define “molecular grade-intermediate” in 1p19q intact IDH-mutant astrocytomas in a 128-patient MSKCC active-surveillance cohort PMID:37910594.





Extremity rhabdomyosarcoma (ARMS) — CDK4 amplification is a recurrent, prognostically adverse event PMID:37315267.





CDK4 amplification is mutually exclusive with CDKN2A deletion in extremity ARMS, consistent with convergent cell-cycle/RB-pathway dysregulation PMID:37315267.





The authors argue CDK4 amplifications should be considered for integration into RMS risk stratification, and raise CDK4/6-directed therapy as a rationale, though efficacy is untested in this cohort PMID:37315267.





Whether CDK4/6 inhibition improves outcomes in CDK4-amplified or CDKN2A-deleted extremity RMS remains untested PMID:37315267.

title: STAT1
text:
STAT1 is a Th1 signaling transcription factor included in the 20-gene Immunologic Constant of Rejection (ICR) immune signature.




STAT1 is a component of the 20-gene ICR panel (Th1 signaling genes) used to stratify colon cancer immune subtypes PMID:37202560.





COAD — ICR panel component; contributes to ICR-high vs ICR-low prognostic stratification (HR 0.54, P=0.0095) PMID:37202560.





Co-expressed with other ICR Th1 genes (IFNG, TBX21, CD8A, IRF1) as a module PMID:37202560.





No direct targeted therapy reported in the corpus; ICR module may have predictive utility for immunotherapy PMID:37202560.





None specific to STAT1 in the corpus.

title: BCL2
text:
BCL2 encodes an anti-apoptotic protein canonically deregulated in B-cell lymphomas via Ig-locus translocations and targeted by venetoclax in CLL.




Recurrent immunoglobulin-locus translocation partner predominantly in IGHV-mutated CLL (5/88 WGS cases, 5.7%), arising via aberrant V(D)J recombination; additional cases identified by WES PMID:35927489.





CLL — BCL2 translocations are an M-CLL-biased structural variant class in the integrated CLL map PMID:35927489.





BCL2 translocations co-segregate with the M-CLL molecular subtype rather than with U-CLL PMID:35927489.





BCL2 is the molecular target of venetoclax, one of the contemporary CLL therapies cited as shifting the prognostic landscape of TP53-mutated disease in the era of ibrutinib/venetoclax PMID:35927489.





Whether BCL2-translocated M-CLL has distinct venetoclax response kinetics is not resolved in the corpus PMID:35927489.





PMID:35927489

title: KRAS
text:
KRAS is a canonical RAS-family oncogene and one of the most frequently mutated drivers in human cancer, with prominent roles in LUAD, colorectal, and pancreatic cancers.




Advanced NSCLC: detection of pathogenic KRAS alterations in ctDNA (vs tissue only) was associated with worse prognosis in the prospective ctDx Lung cohort (n=1,127) PMID:36357680.
Appendiceal adenocarcinoma: KRAS/NRAS mutations in the absence of GNAS and TP53 alterations mark the clinically indolent RAS-mut predominant MAAP subtype with lowest mutational and chromosomal burden and best OS PMID:36493333.
Histiocytosis: KRAS mutated in 7% of profiled cases; one case had co-occurring KRAS G12D and BRAF D594H PMID:36862133.
Female germ cell tumors: KRAS among oncogenic mutations observed in a minority of cases PMID:36862133.
LUAD metastasis: KRAS G12C elevated (21%) in primaries that metastasized to liver vs the matched liver lesions (6%, p<0.001); mutually exclusive with EGFR PMID:37084736.
NSCLC brain metastases: KRAS commonly shared (truncal) between BM and matched primary or extracranial metastasis PMID:37591896.
KRAS was included in LUAD pathway/metastasis analyses in MSK-CHORD (n=24,950) PMID:39506116.
KRAS alterations characterized “biliary-class” intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (along with SMAD4 and CDKN2A loss), associated with markedly worse OS PMID:38864854.





LUAD — KRAS G12C enrichment in liver-metastasizing primaries and truncal KRAS in brain-metastatic trajectories PMID:37084736 PMID:37591896.
APAD — RAS-mut predominant MAAP subtype has best prognosis and 50% first-line chemotherapy response PMID:36493333.
LCH / ECD — KRAS mutated in 7% of histiocytosis PMID:36862133.
Advanced NSCLC — ctDNA-detected KRAS associated with worse prognosis PMID:36357680.





LUAD: KRAS alterations mutually exclusive with EGFR alterations PMID:37084736.
APAD MAAP: defining subtype requires KRAS/NRAS mutation in the absence of GNAS and TP53 PMID:36493333.





ctDNA-guided matching to targeted therapy in NSCLC (including KRAS-directed regimens) yielded OS benefit vs untreated ctDNA-positive patients (HR 0.63, 95% CI 0.52–0.76, P<0.001) PMID:36357680.
RAS-mut predominant MAAP shows 50% first-line chemotherapy response vs 6% in GNAS-mut predominant MAAP (P=.03) PMID:36493333.





Mechanistic basis for apparent loss of KRAS G12C clones in matched liver metastases of LUAD is unresolved PMID:37084736.

title: NKX2-1
text:
NKX2-1 (TTF1) is a lineage-defining transcription factor in lung adenocarcinoma. In the corpus it is altered in metastatic LUAD contexts.




Among genes altered more often in LUAD metastases than matched primaries in the 2,532-specimen MSK metastatic organotropism cohort PMID:37084736.
Enriched in both brain metastases and extracranial metastases of LUAD relative to non-metastatic primaries PMID:37591896.





Lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD) — metastasis-associated alteration PMID:37084736, PMID:37591896.





Enriched in BM+ LUAD PTs alongside TP53, MYC, SMARCA4, RB1, ARID1A, FOXA1 PMID:37591896.





No direct therapeutic link reported in the corpus.





Mechanistic contribution to metastatic tropism vs lineage maintenance is unresolved.

title: NKX2-1
text: NKX2-1 (TTF1) is a lineage-defining transcription factor in lung adenocarcinoma. In the corpus it is altered in metastatic LUAD contexts.

title: NKX2-1
text:
Among genes altered more often in LUAD metastases than matched primaries in the 2,532-specimen MSK metastatic organotropism cohort PMID:37084736.
Enriched in both brain metastases and extracranial metastases of LUAD relative to non-metastatic primaries PMID:37591896.

title: NKX2-1
text: Lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD) — metastasis-associated alteration PMID:37084736, PMID:37591896.

title: NKX2-1
text: Enriched in BM+ LUAD PTs alongside TP53, MYC, SMARCA4, RB1, ARID1A, FOXA1 PMID:37591896.

title: NKX2-1
text: No direct therapeutic link reported in the corpus.

title: NKX2-1
text: Mechanistic contribution to metastatic tropism vs lineage maintenance is unresolved.

title: TSC1
text:
TSC1 encodes hamartin, a tumor suppressor that, with TSC2, restrains mTORC1 signaling. TSC1 loss-of-function is recurrent in urothelial carcinoma and contributes to PI3K/mTOR pathway dysregulation.




TSC1 was altered in 13% (52/414) of FGFR2/3-altered urothelial carcinomas, among the most frequent PI3K-pathway co-alterations PMID:37682528.





BLCA / UTUC — frequent PI3K-pathway co-alteration in FGFR3-altered tumors PMID:37682528.





Co-occurs with FGFR3, PIK3CA, and AKT1 as part of the PI3K-pathway co-alteration spectrum PMID:37682528.





TSC1 co-alteration did not predict response to erdafitinib in FGFR3-altered UC PMID:37682528.





Whether mTOR inhibition has activity in TSC1-altered FGFR3-mutant UC remains untested in the corpus PMID:37682528.





PMID:37682528

title: MET
text:
MET is a receptor tyrosine kinase and an established actionable driver in LUAD via exon 14 skipping and amplification.




Altered more often in LUAD metastases than in matched primaries PMID:37084736.





LUAD — metastasis-enriched alteration in the MSK organotropism cohort (n=2,532) PMID:37084736.





None reported.





None directly evaluated in this cohort.





Whether metastasis-emergent MET alterations in LUAD are clinically actionable at the point of re-biopsy is not resolved; the paper reports only ~4% of metastases carried new actionable alterations overall PMID:37084736.





PMID:37084736

title: EGFR
text:
EGFR encodes the epidermal growth factor receptor tyrosine kinase, one of the best-established oncogenic drivers and therapeutic targets in non-small cell lung cancer.




Pathogenic EGFR alterations detected in plasma ctDNA (vs tissue only) were associated with worse prognosis in advanced NSCLC PMID:36357680.
EGFR alterations were associated with worse metastasis-free survival in LUAD (p=0.002) and mutually exclusive with SMARCA4, STK11, KEAP1, and KRAS alterations PMID:37084736.
Adrenal gland metastases in LUAD had the highest proportion of EGFR activating mutations (133/202, 66%) PMID:37084736.
TP53/EGFR co-alteration with KEAP1 (q<0.001) and STK11 (q<0.001) was significant only in ever-metastatic LUAD primaries PMID:37084736.
In resected NSCLC brain metastases, EGFR alterations were enriched in patients who developed leptomeningeal disease (LMD): 45% vs 21% in non-progressors (p=0.044) PMID:37591896.
LMD patients frequently exhibited uncommon/non-canonical EGFR drivers (L861Q, G719A/S, A755G, N771_H773dup) in 45% of cases, with persistence despite TKI therapy PMID:37591896.
A vignette documented emergence then clearance of T790M under osimertinib while L861Q/G719S drivers persisted PMID:37591896.
EGFR alterations were negatively associated with SETD2 driver mutations in LUAD (q<0.05) in the MSK-CHORD cohort, indicating mutual exclusivity PMID:39506116.





LUAD / LUSC — prognostic via ctDNA detection and organotropism; worse MFS PMID:36357680 PMID:37084736.
NSCLC — non-canonical drivers enriched in leptomeningeal disease PMID:37591896.





Mutually exclusive with SMARCA4, STK11, KEAP1, KRAS in LUAD PMID:37084736.
Co-altered with TP53 in ever-metastatic LUAD, further combined with KEAP1/STK11 PMID:37084736.





ctDNA-guided matching to EGFR-targeted therapy yielded longer OS in advanced NSCLC (HR 0.63) PMID:36357680.
Non-canonical EGFR mutations in brain metastases may identify patients at elevated risk for LMD and partial resistance to osimertinib; prior erlotinib exposure featured in EGFR-mutant LMD trajectories PMID:37591896.





Whether non-canonical EGFR drivers cause leptomeningeal tropism or merely reflect osimertinib partial resistance is unresolved PMID:37591896.
Whether ctDNA-only subclonal resistance alterations should directly alter EGFR-directed treatment selection PMID:36357680.





PMID:36357680
PMID:37084736
PMID:37591896
PMID:39506116

title: PAX3
text:
PAX3 is a paired-box transcription factor. PAX3::FOXO1 fusions define the adverse-prognosis subset of alveolar rhabdomyosarcoma.




PAX3::FOXO1 fusion present in 70% of extremity alveolar RMS cases in the 61-patient MSK cohort; detected by Archer FusionPlex or FISH PMID:37315267.
PAX3::FOXO1 ARMS had worse prognosis and affected older children than PAX7::FOXO1 (HR 3.45, P=.016) PMID:37315267.





Alveolar rhabdomyosarcoma (ARMS) — dominant fusion partner; adverse prognosis PMID:37315267.





PAX3::FOXO1 and PAX7::FOXO1 are alternative, mutually exclusive fusion events in ARMS PMID:37315267.





Fusion-partner-aware risk stratification is proposed; no direct targeted therapy reported PMID:37315267.





Mechanism of PAX3 vs PAX7 fusion-specific prognostic difference PMID:37315267.





PMID:37315267

title: RUNX1
text:
RUNX1 encodes a master hematopoietic transcription factor; germline and somatic mutations are recurrent across myeloid malignancies and confer hematologic malignancy risk in clonal hematopoiesis.




RUNX1 was identified as a high-risk clonal hematopoiesis (CH) genotype conferring particularly high risk of subsequent hematologic malignancy in 42,714 MSK patients with nonhematologic cancer profiled by MSK-IMPACT matched-normal sequencing PMID:38147626.





Clonal hematopoiesis in patients with nonhematologic cancer — RUNX1-mutant CH anticipates progression to hematologic malignancy PMID:38147626.





Not specifically reported in the corpus.





RUNX1-mutant CH may warrant closer hematologic surveillance in solid-tumor patients PMID:38147626.





None flagged in the corpus.





PMID:38147626

title: Cancer Therapy and Clonal Hematopoiesis, MSK, Blood Adv 2023
text:
Cohort of 42,714 MSK patients with nonhematologic cancer who had matched-normal peripheral blood sequencing as the germline comparator for MSK-IMPACT tumor profiling. Built to characterize clonal hematopoiesis (CH) genotypes — including “silent” synonymous/noncoding variants — associated with subsequent hematologic malignancy PMID:38147626.




42,714 patients with nonhematologic cancer profiled by MSK-IMPACT; 39,510 were alive without a hematologic diagnosis within 2 weeks of sequencing and had follow-up beyond that PMID:38147626.
Hematologic malignancy events identified via a hybrid NLP (CEDARS) + manual chart review pipeline, adjudicated by board-certified hematologists/oncologists PMID:38147626.
26 patients had paired post-diagnosis sequencing for VAF trajectory analysis PMID:38147626.





MSK-IMPACT — hybridization-capture targeted sequencing, 341–505 genes PMID:38147626.





PMID:38147626 — Stonestrom et al., High-risk and silent clonal hematopoietic genotypes in patients with nonhematologic cancer, Blood Advances 2024.





Any CH conferred HR = 1.93 (95% CI 1.45–2.57, P<.001) for subsequent hematologic malignancy after adjustment for age, sex, and solid cancer type PMID:38147626.
JAK2, RUNX1, and XPO1 identified as high-risk CH genotypes PMID:38147626.
Silent CH (synonymous/noncoding) predicts hematologic malignancy risk, especially with multiple variants or high VAF PMID:38147626.
At hematologic malignancy onset, JAK2 and TP53 VAFs expanded while DNMT3A and silent CH VAFs mostly decreased PMID:38147626.





cBioPortal study msk_ch_2023 PMID:38147626.

title: Metastatic Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (MSK, Nature Medicine 2022)
text:
Prospective international cohort study of advanced NSCLC patients undergoing plasma ctDNA sequencing via the Resolution Bioscience ctDx Lung platform, evaluating ctDNA detection as a prognostic and therapy-matching biomarker PMID:36357680.




1,127 adults with stage IV or recurrent NSCLC analyzed (1,357 initially enrolled) at Memorial Sloan Kettering (New York) and GenesisCare (Sydney, Australia); cancer types LUAD and LUSC; most (1,003; 89%) provided a single plasma sample PMID:36357680.





ctDx Lung targeted plasma NGS; MSK patients additionally profiled with tissue MSK-IMPACT, and from June 2019 with plasma MSK-ACCESS; both MSK assays use matched WBC sequencing to remove clonal hematopoiesis and germline variants PMID:36357680.





PMID:36357680 — Jee et al., Overall survival with ctDNA-guided therapy in advanced NSCLC.





ctDNA detection rate was 722/1,127 (64%), and ctDNA detection was an independent poor prognostic marker (HR 2.05; 95% CI 1.74–2.42; P<0.001) even after correction for clinicopathologic factors and PET-based metabolic tumor volume PMID:36357680.
Among ctDNA-positive patients, 255 (23%) matched to targeted therapy by ctDNA sequencing had longer OS than ctDNA-positive patients not receiving targeted therapy (HR 0.63; 95% CI 0.52–0.76; P<0.001) PMID:36357680.
25% of patients had alterations detected only in ctDNA (not in time-matched tissue), disproportionately subclonal resistance drivers such as RICTOR and PIK3CA, and associated with short survival PMID:36357680.
Operational metrics: only 2% of ctDx Lung / MSK-ACCESS samples failed vs 13% tissue sequencing failures; turnaround 11 days vs 33 days for MSK-IMPACT PMID:36357680.





cBioPortal study nsclc_ctdx_msk_2022 PMID:36357680.

title: Mature T and NK Neoplasms (MSK, Blood Adv 2023)
text:
Retrospective MSK cohort of adults with nodal peripheral T-cell lymphoma treated with curative-intent CHOP-based chemotherapy, profiled with MSK-IMPACT to identify somatic predictors of outcome PMID:37078708.




132 adults with nodal PTCL (PTCL-NOS, AITL, PTCL-TFH, ALK± ALCL) plus EATL and MEITL, treated at MSK 2015–2020 PMID:37078708.
Histology mix: AITL 47%, PTCL-NOS 27%, ALK− ALCL 11%, PTCL-TFH 7%, ALK+ ALCL 5%, MEITL 3%; nested cohorts for complete clinical data (n=87) and prospective analysis (n=72) PMID:37078708.





MSK-IMPACT across IMPACT341 / IMPACT410 / IMPACT468 versions depending on era PMID:37078708.





PMID:37078708 — Johnson et al., TP53 mutations identify high-risk events for PTCL treated with CHOP-based chemotherapy.





Most frequently mutated genes: TET2 52%, RHOA 30%, DNMT3A 19%, TP53 16%, IDH2 11%; in AITL, 88% of RHOA mutations were canonical G17V PMID:37078708.
TP53 mutations (HR 3.1; 95% CI 1.4–6.8; P=.005) and TP53/17p deletions (HR 4.1; 95% CI 1.1–15.0; P=.03) were the only somatic aberrancies correlating with inferior PFS; median PFS 4.5 vs 10.5 months (TP53-mut vs TP53-wt) PMID:37078708.
CDKN2A deletions (n=9) correlated with inferior OS (HR 12.1; 95% CI 2.8–52.0; P<.001); 3/4 MEITL cases carried CDKN2A aberrancies PMID:37078708.





cBioPortal study mtnn_msk_2022 PMID:37078708.

title: Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumor Genomic Risk Stratification, MSK, Clin Cancer Res 2023
text:
MSKCC cohort of 501 GIST patients (592 samples) diagnosed 1991–2021 profiled by MSK-IMPACT targeted sequencing. Used to build an elastic-net penalized Cox machine learning model producing a 3-tier genomic risk stratification for recurrence-free survival in primary localized gastric and small bowel GISTs in the adjuvant imatinib era PMID:37477937.




501 patients / 592 samples: 275 gastric (307 samples), 194 small bowel (244 samples), 32 rectal (41 samples; excluded from modeling) PMID:37477937.
Modeling cohort: 152 primary localized gastric and 80 small bowel GISTs; 41% of gastric and 53% of small bowel received adjuvant imatinib PMID:37477937.
Cancer type: GIST.





MSK-IMPACT — targeted DNA NGS, 341–505 genes, matched tumor-normal PMID:37477937.





PMID:37477937 — Dermawan et al., Novel Genomic Risk Stratification Model for Primary GIST in the Adjuvant Therapy Era, Clin Cancer Res 2023.





Gastric GIST high-risk markers: chr1p deletion or SDHB loss; intermediate: chr14q deletion or absence of KIT exon 11 mutation PMID:37477937.
Small bowel GIST high-risk markers: MAX/MGA/MYC, CDKN2A, or RB1 alterations; intermediate: chr1p deletion or chr5q amplification PMID:37477937.
Genomic model both upgrades and downgrades patients vs Miettinen/NIH-Fletcher/Joensuu schemes, implying conventional models may mis-stratify adjuvant-era patients PMID:37477937.
In 26 SDH-deficient GISTs, TP53 mutations or chr1q amplification portend worse RFS/DSS PMID:37477937.





cBioPortal study gist_msk_2023 PMID:37477937.

title: Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumor Genomic Risk Stratification, MSK, Clin Cancer Res 2023
text: PMID:37477937 — Dermawan et al., Novel Genomic Risk Stratification Model for Primary GIST in the Adjuvant Therapy Era, Clin Cancer Res 2023.

title: Gastrointestinal Stromal Tumor Genomic Risk Stratification, MSK, Clin Cancer Res 2023
text:
Gastric GIST high-risk markers: chr1p deletion or SDHB loss; intermediate: chr14q deletion or absence of KIT exon 11 mutation PMID:37477937.
Small bowel GIST high-risk markers: MAX/MGA/MYC, CDKN2A, or RB1 alterations; intermediate: chr1p deletion or chr5q amplification PMID:37477937.
Genomic model both upgrades and downgrades patients vs Miettinen/NIH-Fletcher/Joensuu schemes, implying conventional models may mis-stratify adjuvant-era patients PMID:37477937.
In 26 SDH-deficient GISTs, TP53 mutations or chr1q amplification portend worse RFS/DSS PMID:37477937.

title: Appendiceal Cancer (MSK, J Clin Oncol 2022)
text:
Retrospective MSK cohort of patients with appendiceal adenocarcinoma (AC) sequenced on MSK-IMPACT between April 2015 and October 2020, released as the cBioPortal study appendiceal_msk_2022 PMID:36493333.




Appendiceal adenocarcinoma (APAD); molecular-subtype analysis focused on 164 mucinous appendiceal adenocarcinoma (MAAP) tumors, extended to goblet cell (GCA) and colonic-type (CTAAP) appendiceal adenocarcinomas PMID:36493333.





Matched tumor/normal hybridization-capture NGS via MSK-IMPACT; oncogenic/actionable calls via OncoKB; clonality via FACETS PMID:36493333.





PMID:36493333 — Foote et al., Molecular Classification of Appendiceal Adenocarcinoma.





Three MAAP molecular subtypes defined by co-occurring mutations in GNAS, KRAS/NRAS, and TP53: a RAS-mut predominant indolent subtype, a GNAS-mut predominant chemo-resistant subtype, and a TP53-mut predominant highly aneuploid aggressive subtype PMID:36493333.
OS was significantly better in RAS-mut predominant patients than GNAS-mut (P=.05) or TP53-mut (P=.004) subtypes; first-line chemotherapy response was 50% in RAS-mut vs 6% in GNAS-mut predominant MAAP (P=.03) PMID:36493333.
Tumor aneuploidy was independently associated with poor prognosis (P=.001) in multivariable models adjusting for histology, grade, age, CRS number, and first-line chemotherapy PMID:36493333.





cBioPortal study appendiceal_msk_2022 PMID:36493333.
